11	type Config struct {

13		Harness         string              `yaml:"harness"`          // optional forced harness override
14		Model           string              `yaml:"model"`            // optional default model ref or exact pin
15		Models          map[string]string   `yaml:"models"`           // per-harness model overrides
16		ReasoningLevels map[string][]string `yaml:"reasoning_levels"` // per-harness reasoning-level options
17		TimeoutMS       int                 `yaml:"timeout_ms"`       // idle (inactivity) timeout in ms — resets on every stream/event
18		WallClockMS     int                 `yaml:"wall_clock_ms"`    // absolute wall-clock cap in ms — fires regardless of activity
19		SessionLogDir   string              `yaml:"session_log_dir"`  // log directory
20		Permissions     string              `yaml:"permissions"`      // permission level: safe, supervised, unrestricted

35	// RunOptions holds options for a single agent invocation.

37		// Context is the caller's context. When non-nil, the Runner derives its
38		// internal cancel context from this so upstream cancellation (e.g.
39		// server.WorkerManager.Stop) propagates into the provider HTTP call.
40		// Nil defaults to context.Background().
41		Context       context.Context
42		Harness       string
43		Prompt        string // prompt text (or path to file)
44		PromptFile    string // explicit file path
45		PromptSource  string
46		Correlation   map[string]string
47		Model         string
48		Provider      string // explicit provider name (e.g. "vidar", "openrouter"); bypasses default provider selection
49		ModelRef      string // catalog model-ref (e.g. "code-medium"); resolved via the model catalog
50		Effort        string
51		Timeout       time.Duration // idle (inactivity) timeout; nonzero overrides Config.TimeoutMS
52		WallClock     time.Duration // absolute wall-clock cap; nonzero overrides Config.WallClockMS
53		WorkDir       string

58	// RunArgs is the SD-024 Stage 2 adapter type that production callers
59	// migrate to in subsequent B22 beads. It collapses the durable knobs
60	// (currently sourced from config.ResolvedConfig in RunWithConfig) and
61	// the per-invocation plumbing (currently in AgentRunRuntime) into a
62	// single flat struct, mirroring RunOptions, so callers don't need to
63	// assemble a ResolvedConfig just to dispatch a single agent run.
64	//
65	// The type is declared here so that B22b/c/d (external-caller
66	// migration beads) and the eventual B22 final removal can reference
67	// it. In this bead it is not yet wired through Run; runInternal still
68	// accepts RunOptions and the public Run signature is preserved.
69	type RunArgs struct {
70		Context       context.Context
71		Harness       string
72		Prompt        string
73		PromptFile    string
74		PromptSource  string
75		Correlation   map[string]string
76		Model         string
77		Provider      string
78		ModelRef      string
79		Effort        string
80		Timeout       time.Duration
81		WallClock     time.Duration
82		WorkDir       string
83		Permissions   string
84		SessionLogDir string
85	}

415	// Default configuration values.
416	const (
417		DefaultHarness = "codex"
418		// DefaultTimeoutMS is the default idle (inactivity) timeout — resets on
419		// every stdout/stderr byte or agent event. 2 hours is long enough for any
420		// task where the provider streams progress; a stuck provider still needs
421		// DefaultWallClockMS to guarantee termination.
422		DefaultTimeoutMS = 7200000 // 2 hours
423		// DefaultWallClockMS is the absolute wall-clock cap applied in addition to
424		// the idle timeout. It fires regardless of stream/event activity, so a
425		// provider that emits heartbeats forever cannot pin a worker indefinitely.
426		// Sized at 3h — roughly 1.5x the idle timeout — so normal long tasks still
427		// complete while genuinely hung workers free themselves. See
428		// RC2 of ddx-0a651925 for the incident that motivated this bound.
429		DefaultWallClockMS = 10800000 // 3 hours
430		DefaultLogDir      = ".ddx/agent-logs"
431	)
432	
433	// ResolveLogDir returns an absolute session-log directory path anchored at
434	// projectRoot. Callers that construct an agent.Runner must use this to avoid
435	// the Runner's relative DefaultLogDir resolving against process CWD — which
436	// historically wrote logs to stray locations like cli/internal/server/.ddx/.
437	func ResolveLogDir(projectRoot, configured string) string {
438		if configured == "" {
439			configured = DefaultLogDir
440		}
441		if filepath.IsAbs(configured) {
442			return configured
443		}
444		if projectRoot == "" {
445			return configured
446		}
447		return filepath.Join(projectRoot, configured)
448	}

7	// harnessToSurface maps harness names to their catalog surface identifier.
8	// This is the only place harness→surface is declared; all model resolution
9	// goes through BuiltinCatalog using the surface name.
10	var harnessToSurface = map[string]string{
11		"codex":    "codex",
12		"claude":   "claude",
13		"agent":    "embedded-openai",
14		"opencode": "claude",
15	}

17	// ResolveModelTier returns the concrete model for a given harness and tier
18	// by looking up the tier profile in BuiltinCatalog for the harness's surface.
19	func ResolveModelTier(harness string, tier escalation.ModelTier) string {
20		surface, ok := harnessToSurface[harness]
21		if !ok {
22			return ""
23		}
24		model, _ := BuiltinCatalog.Resolve(string(tier), surface)
25		return model
26	}

36	// DefaultBenchmarkArms returns the standard set of arms for a full comparison.
37	func DefaultBenchmarkArms() []BenchmarkArm {
38		return []BenchmarkArm{

# Lefthook configuration for DDx project
# Fast, cross-platform git hooks
# https://github.com/evilmartians/lefthook

# Configuration
colors: true
no_tty: false
assertion: false

# Reusable command groups
command_groups:
  go_checks: &go_checks
    root: "cli/"  # Go module root
    tags: [go, backend]

  security_checks: &security_checks
    tags: [security]

  formatting_checks: &formatting_checks
    tags: [format, style]

# ====================
# Pre-commit hooks - Fast checks on staged files
# ====================
pre-commit:
  parallel: true

  commands:
    # Conflict detection using grep on staged files only.
    # Uses git diff --cached directly to avoid {staged_files} tty/expansion issues
    # when running detached (e.g. ddx work --local, no controlling tty).
    conflicts:
      name: "Check merge conflicts"
      run: |
        files=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR 2>/dev/null)
        [ -z "$files" ] && exit 0
        conflict_file=$(printf '%s\n' "$files" | while IFS= read -r f; do
          [ -f "$f" ] || continue
          grep -lE "^<<<<<<<[ 	]|^=======$|^>>>>>>>[ 	]|^\|{7}[ 	]" "$f" 2>/dev/null && break
        done | head -1)
        if [ -n "$conflict_file" ]; then
          echo "Conflict markers found in: $conflict_file"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      fail_text: "Merge conflict markers found. Resolve conflicts before committing."

    # Debug statements - using ripgrep if available, fallback to grep
    # Note: Disabled because CLI applications legitimately use fmt.Print for output
    # debug-go:
    #   name: "Debug statements (Go)"
    #   glob: "*.go"
    #   exclude: "*_test.go"
    #   run: |
    #     if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    #       rg -q "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     else
    #       grep -E "(fmt\.Print|println|spew\.Dump|pprof\.|debug\.Print)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
    #     fi
    #   fail_text: "Debug statements found in Go files"

    debug-python:
      name: "Debug statements (Python)"
      glob: "*.py"
      run: |
        if command -v rg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          rg -q "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} && exit 1 || exit 0
        else
          grep -E "(print\(|pdb\.set_trace|breakpoint\(\)|import pdb)" {staged_files} 2>/dev/null && exit 1 || exit 0
        fi
      fail_text: "Debug statements found in Python files"

    # Go formatting - native gofmt
    go-fmt:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Format"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        unformatted=$(gofmt -l {staged_files})
        if [ -n "$unformatted" ]; then
          echo "Files need formatting: $unformatted"
          echo "Run: gofmt -w <files>"
          exit 1
        fi
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase

    # Go linting - golangci-lint required
    go-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Lint"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        if ! command -v golangci-lint >/dev/null 2>&1; then
          echo "❌ golangci-lint is required but not installed"
          echo "Run: scripts/dev-setup.sh"
          exit 1
        fi
        # Run on packages containing staged files, not individual files.

# SD-024 Stage 4 runtime-lint: forbidden durable-knob fields on
    # *Runtime structs and reintroduction of legacy *Options types.
    # Scoped to packages containing staged files, with diagnostics
    # filtered to only those staged files so existing violations in
    # untouched packages don't block unrelated commits. The full-tree
    # gate in the `ci` block fires once the RunOptions/CompareOptions/
    # QuorumOptions retirement beads land.
    runtime-lint:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Runtime Lint (SD-024 forbidden runtime fields + legacy options)"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        staged=$(git diff --cached --name-only --diff-filter=ACMR | grep -E '^cli/.+\.go$' || true)
        [ -z "$staged" ] && exit 0
        pkgs=$(printf '%s\n' "$staged" | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/|./|' | grep -v '^\./tools/lint/runtimelint' || true)
        [ -z "$pkgs" ] && exit 0
        out=$(go run ./tools/lint/runtimelint/cmd/runtimelint $pkgs 2>&1) || true
        violations=""
        for f in $staged; do
          bare="${f#cli/}"
          m=$(printf '%s\n' "$out" | grep "/$bare:" || true)
          if [ -n "$m" ]; then
            violations="$violations$m"$'\n'
          fi
        done
        if [ -n "$violations" ]; then
          printf '%s' "$violations"
          exit 1
        fi
        exit 0
      skip:
        - merge
        - rebase
      fail_text: "Forbidden runtime-struct field or legacy *Options type in staged file. Move durable knobs to config.ResolvedConfig and use the *Runtime pair (SD-024 §Stage 4 / TD-024 §Lint rule)."

    # Go tests - targeted testing
    go-test:
      <<: *go_checks
      name: "Go Tests"
      glob: "cli/**/*.go"
      run: |
        packages=$(echo {staged_files} | xargs -n1 dirname | sort -u | sed 's|^cli/||')
        for pkg in $packages; do
          if ls "$pkg"/*_test.go >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            go test -short -race "./$pkg"
          fi
        done

18	// TestParseClaudeStream feeds synthetic stream-json events through the
19	// parser and asserts:
20	//
21	//  1. Progress lines (session.start, llm.response, tool.call) are emitted
22	//     for relevant events so the existing TailSessionLogs pipeline can
23	//     render them.
24	//  2. The final aggregated result captures the text, tokens, cost, and
25	//     session id from the authoritative "result" event.
26	//  3. The parser stays lenient in the face of garbage lines.
27	//
28	// The table-driven form exercises multiple event shapes so regressions in
29	// one case don't hide breakage in another.
30	func TestParseClaudeStream(t *testing.T) {
31		cases := []struct {
32			name              string
33			input             string
34			wantTurnCount     int
35			wantToolCalls     int
36			wantInputTokens   int
37			wantOutputTokens  int
38			wantCostUSD       float64
39			wantFinalText     string
40			wantSessionID     string
41			wantModel         string
42			wantProgressTypes []string
43		}{
44			{
45				name: "full stream with tool use and result",
46				input: strings.Join([]string{
47					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-abc","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","tools":["Bash","Read"]}`,
48					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-1","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Starting"},{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-1","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"ls"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":120,"output_tokens":42}}}`,
49					`{"type":"user","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_result","tool_use_id":"tu-1","content":"README.md\nfoo.go"}]}}`,
50					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"m-2","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6","content":[{"type":"text","text":"Done."}],"usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88}}}`,
51					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","is_error":false,"duration_ms":1200,"result":"All done.","usage":{"input_tokens":260,"output_tokens":88},"total_cost_usd":0.0123,"session_id":"sess-abc"}`,
52				}, "\n"),
53				wantTurnCount:     2,
54				wantToolCalls:     1,
55				wantInputTokens:   260,
56				wantOutputTokens:  88,
57				wantCostUSD:       0.0123,
58				wantFinalText:     "All done.",
59				wantSessionID:     "sess-abc",
60				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
61				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call", "llm.response"},
62			},
63			{
64				name: "garbage lines are skipped",
65				input: strings.Join([]string{
66					`not json`,
67					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-xyz","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
68					`{garbage`,
69					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"tu-2","name":"Read","input":{"path":"/tmp/x"}}],"usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5}}}`,
70					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"ok","usage":{"input_tokens":10,"output_tokens":5},"total_cost_usd":0.001,"session_id":"sess-xyz"}`,
71				}, "\n"),
72				wantTurnCount:     1,
73				wantToolCalls:     1,
74				wantInputTokens:   10,
75				wantOutputTokens:  5,
76				wantCostUSD:       0.001,
77				wantFinalText:     "ok",
78				wantSessionID:     "sess-xyz",
79				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
80				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response", "tool.call"},
81			},
82			{
83				name: "text-only assistant without tool_use still emits progress",
84				input: strings.Join([]string{
85					`{"type":"system","subtype":"init","session_id":"sess-t","model":"claude-sonnet-4-6"}`,
86					`{"type":"assistant","message":{"content":[{"type":"text","text":"hello"}],"usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2}}}`,
87					`{"type":"result","subtype":"success","result":"hello","usage":{"input_tokens":3,"output_tokens":2},"total_cost_usd":0.0001,"session_id":"sess-t"}`,
88				}, "\n"),
89				wantTurnCount:     1,
90				wantToolCalls:     0,
91				wantInputTokens:   3,
92				wantOutputTokens:  2,
93				wantCostUSD:       0.0001,
94				wantFinalText:     "hello",
95				wantSessionID:     "sess-t",
96				wantModel:         "claude-sonnet-4-6",
97				wantProgressTypes: []string{"session.start", "llm.response"},
98			},
99		}
100	
101		for _, tc := range cases {
102			tc := tc
103			t.Run(tc.name, func(t *testing.T) {
104				var progressBuf bytes.Buffer
105				start := time.Now().Add(-3 * time.Second) // exercise elapsed_ms > 0
106				res, err := parseClaudeStream(strings.NewReader(tc.input), &progressBuf, "sess-test", "ddx-12345678", start)
107				require.NoError(t, err)
108				require.NotNil(t, res)
109	
110				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantTurnCount, res.TurnCount, "turn count")
111				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantToolCalls, res.ToolCalls, "tool call count")
112				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantInputTokens, res.InputTokens, "input tokens")
113				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantOutputTokens, res.OutputTokens, "output tokens")
114				assert.InDelta(t, tc.wantCostUSD, res.CostUSD, 1e-9, "cost usd")
115				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantFinalText, res.FinalText, "final text")
116				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantSessionID, res.SessionID, "session id")
117				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantModel, res.Model, "model")
118				assert.False(t, res.IsError)
119	
120				// Walk the emitted progress lines and confirm the expected types
121				// appear in order.
122				var gotTypes []string
123				for _, line := range strings.Split(strings.TrimSpace(progressBuf.String()), "\n") {
124					if line == "" {
125						continue
126					}
127					var entry map[string]any
128					require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry), "progress line must be valid JSON: %s", line)
129					t, _ := entry["type"].(string)
130					gotTypes = append(gotTypes, t)
131				}
132				assert.Equal(t, tc.wantProgressTypes, gotTypes, "progress event types (in order)")
133	
134				// Spot-check that at least one tool.call line carries the bead_id
135				// so execute-bead operators can grep the log stream.
136				if tc.wantToolCalls > 0 {
137					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"bead_id":"ddx-12345678"`)
138					assert.Contains(t, progressBuf.String(), `"tool.call"`)
139				}
140			})
141		}
142	}

257	func findClaudeProgressLog(t *testing.T, dir string) string {

input.content:
Bead ddx-ce7a796e (B22d-g) cannot be landed in isolation against base
41d464b03c4e5d12b7a9244c3c59ee312e64e255. Same fundamental blocker as
B22d-f (ddx-9f4b1a8c, no_changes 2026-04-27): lefthook `runtime-lint`
hook (lefthook.yml) filters analyzer diagnostics to the set of staged
files, and the in-scope test files contain pre-existing RunOptions /
CompareOptions composite literals whose underlying APIs are explicitly
named out-of-scope by this bead's parent decomposition (step h owns
service_run.go; runner.go and claude_stream.go aren't in this step's
in-scope at all).

What is done (verified locally, then reverted)
- compare_adapter.go is straightforward to drain of RunOptions /
  CompareOptions / QuorumOptions:
    * RunFunc redeclared as `func(rcfg config.ResolvedConfig,
      runtime AgentRunRuntime) (*Result, error)`.
    * defaultResolvePromptForCompare(runtime AgentRunRuntime).
    * runCompareArmWith(run RunFunc, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime,
      armIdx, harnessName, baseDir, prompt, worktreePath).
    * RunCompareWith(run, rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime, resolvePrompt,
      cleanupSandbox).
    * RunQuorumWith(run, rcfg, runtime QuorumRuntime).
    * RunBenchmarkWith(runCompare func(config.ResolvedConfig,
      CompareRuntime) (*ComparisonRecord, error), suite).
    * RunCompareViaService / RunCompareWithAgent / RunQuorumViaService /
      RunQuorumWithAgent / RunBenchmarkViaService / RunBenchmarkWithAgent
      rewritten to take (rcfg, runtime) instead of CompareOptions /
      QuorumOptions and to delegate through the new RunFunc.
    * RunCompareWithConfigViaService / RunQuorumWithConfigViaService
      collapsed into a single forwarding hop (they no longer need to
      assemble a CompareOptions/QuorumOptions intermediary).
    * RunQuorumWithConfig retained, now calling RunQuorumWith with the
      new shape.
- models.go BenchmarkArmsToCompare changes return shape from
  CompareOptions to CompareRuntime (and accepts an AgentRunRuntime
  baseline rather than a RunOptions). compare_adapter.go's
  RunBenchmarkWith uses that shape directly.
- models_test.go updates the BenchmarkArmsToCompare call to pass
  `AgentRunRuntime{Prompt: "test"}` and assert
  CompareRuntime.{Harnesses, ArmModels, ArmLabels}. This is the only
  RunOptions-related drift in models_test.go — the rest of that file
  doesn't reference RunOptions.
- After the in-package edits, `cd cli && go build ./...` passes and
  `cd cli && go test ./internal/agent/...` is clean once the test
  files in the next bullet are also updated.
- agent_test.go (out of scope) needs one closure-shape update at
  line 584 because RunFunc's signature changed; with that one-line
  diff applied locally, TestQuorumRunsAllHarnesses passes.

What is blocking (lint policy vs. bead scope)

1. claude_stream_test.go (in-scope) contains RunOptions composite
   literals at lines 206, 212, 218, 224, 291, and 324. These are all
   inside test cases that call helpers declared in claude_stream.go —
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir(opts RunOptions, cfg Config) at
   claude_stream.go:284 and runClaudeStreamingFn(r, ctx, harness,
   "claude", "", opts RunOptions, "hi", "", 10*time.Second) at
   claude_stream.go:304/477/507. claude_stream.go is NOT in this
   bead's in-scope; B22d's per-step decomposition reserves it for a
   separate step. With claude_stream.go's RunOptions surface
   unchanged, the test cannot construct anything other than
   RunOptions{...} to drive resolveClaudeProgressLogDir, and the
   runtime-lint hook flags every one of those literals when the
   file is staged.

2. prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go (in-scope) has three RunOptions
   composite literals:
     * line 78  — r.resolvePrompt(RunOptions{PromptFile: fixture})
                  drives Runner.resolvePrompt in runner.go:976.
                  runner.go is out of scope.
     * line 85  — defaultResolvePromptForCompare(RunOptions{...})
                  drives the in-scope helper. CAN be updated.
     * line 112 — RunViaServiceWith(ctx, svc, dir, RunOptions{...})
                  drives service_run.go:104. service_run.go's public
                  API is explicitly named out-of-scope ("step h").
   Even after migrating line 85 and updating line 98's RunBenchmarkWith
   closure to the new (rcfg, runtime CompareRuntime) shape, the file
   still carries lines 78 and 112 as pre-existing RunOptions
   composite literals. runtime-lint scopes diagnostics by file (not
   by hunk), so the staged file fails the gate on lines that this
   bead is not authorized to touch.

3. agent_test.go (out of scope, but transitively required) — once
   RunFunc's signature changes, agent_test.go:584's
   `func(opts RunOptions) (*Result, error)` no longer matches
   RunQuorumWithConfig's RunFunc parameter, so the package fails to
   build. The minimal fix is one line, but agent_test.go has 18
   other RunOptions composite literals (BuildArgs call sites at
   lines 118, 130, 148, 157, 165, 188, 196, 209, 214, 677, 684,
   718, 727, 856, 864, 971, plus 678, 684) that are pre-existing,
   unrelated to this bead, and depend on BuildArgs (runner.go) being
   migrated separately. Staging agent_test.go would surface every
   one of those as a lint violation.

The runtime-lint hook is mandatory (per project policy: --no-verify is
forbidden). Diagnostic filtering is whole-file, not hunk-aware, so
"only edit my own lines" doesn't help when the file already contained
violations that this bead's named scope can't reach.

What a follow-up attempt would need (any of the following)

A. Re-decompose B22d-g to also include claude_stream.go (so
   resolveClaudeProgressLogDir / runClaudeStreamingFn migrate off
   RunOptions in the same commit, draining claude_stream_test.go),
   AND service_run.go's RunViaServiceWith + runner.go's
   resolvePrompt (so prompt_ingress_oversize_test.go drains), AND
   agent_test.go's BuildArgs call sites (so the RunFunc signature
   change compiles cleanly across the package). This is essentially
   the previously-attempted atomic B22d scope (worker-3f7a's
   recommendation, applied 2026-04-26 in commit 97561a6e); the
   subsequent re-decomposition into steps a-h reintroduced this
   exact tension.

B. Land step h FIRST (and in fact most of the rest of the
   per-file decomposition) so that claude_stream.go, runner.go,
   service_run.go, and agent_test.go no longer contain pre-existing
   RunOptions usages, then run step g — at that point the in-scope
   test files have no out-of-scope violations and lint passes on the
   staged set. The current bead-queue ordering puts step g before
   step h, so this requires a tracker reordering.

C. Change runtime-lint's filter from whole-file to hunk-scoped (so
   pre-existing violations on lines this bead doesn't touch don't
   block the commit). This is a lint-policy change in lefthook.yml;
   not in this bead's scope and not authorized by the bead
   description.

D. Authorize the worker (model-side) to extend in-scope to cover
   the transitively-required files (claude_stream.go,
   runner.go.resolvePrompt + BuildArgs, service_run.go.RunViaServiceWith,
   and agent_test.go), the same way B22d itself was authorized in
   2026-04-26 (commit 97561a6e). The diff for that expansion is
   already prepared and verified locally; only the bead-scope
   constraint stands in the way.

The implementation is mechanical and verified locally; the blocker is
project-level lint policy versus per-step bead scope, not
implementation difficulty. No partial commit is justified, since
landing only the in-scope-clean half (compare_adapter.go +
models.go + models_test.go) breaks the package build by leaving
agent_test.go with an incompatible RunFunc closure, and updating
that closure trips the lint on agent_test.go's pre-existing 18+
RunOptions literals.
